name: frontend-code-review
description: Trigger when the user requests a review of frontend files (e.g., `.tsx`, `.ts`, `.js`). Support both pending-change reviews and focused file reviews while applying the checklist rules.
capabilities: Review TypeScript and JavaScript files (TSX, JSX) using a comprehensive checklist
Check code quality rules including type safety, naming conventions, and component patterns
Identify business logic issues such as error handling gaps and missing validations
Evaluate performance concerns like unnecessary re-renders and expensive operations
author: langgenius
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static scanner flagged text patterns in markdown files (backticks for code formatting, URLs in references, technical terms in documentation). All 45 detected patterns are FALSE POSITIVES - markdown documentation cannot execute commands, make network calls, or contain exploitable code. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Frontend code reviews help developers catch bugs and anti-patterns before deployment. This skill provides automated checklist-based reviews for TypeScript, JavaScript, TSX, and JSX files using industry best practices.
limitations: Only supports frontend file types (TSX, TS, JSX, JS) - does not review backend code
Does not execute or test code - provides static analysis only
Does not modify files - only reports findings without making changes

useCases.targetUser: Developers needing fast feedback on specific files
useCases.title: Quick File Review
useCases.description: Request a focused review of a single file to get immediate feedback on code quality and potential issues.

useCases.title: Pull Request Review
useCases.description: Review all changed files in a pull request to ensure code meets quality standards before merging.

useCases.title: Legacy Code Assessment
useCases.description: Evaluate older codebases to identify technical debt and prioritize refactoring efforts.

promptTemplates.title: Basic File Review
promptTemplates.prompt:
Review this file for code quality and potential issues:

${file_path}
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick review of a single file

promptTemplates.title: Changed Files Review
promptTemplates.prompt:
Review these changed files for a pull request. Apply the full checklist from business-logic.md, code-quality.md, and performance.md:

${file_paths}

promptTemplates.title: Focus on Specific Issues
promptTemplates.prompt:
Review this file with emphasis on business logic and error handling:

${file_path}

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Review
promptTemplates.prompt:
Perform a complete code review of the following files. For each issue found, explain the problem and suggest improvements:

${file_paths}

bestPractices: Use the skill for both focused single-file reviews and comprehensive multi-file assessments to catch different types of issues
Review findings in context - some suggestions may not apply to your specific use case or coding standards
Combine automated reviews with manual testing to ensure code works correctly in addition to being well-structured
antiPatterns: Avoid requesting reviews of non-frontend files (Python, Go, etc.) - the skill is optimized for TS/JS ecosystems
Do not expect the skill to execute or test your code - it provides static analysis only
Avoid ignoring findings related to TypeScript types - strict typing prevents runtime errors

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports TypeScript (.ts), JavaScript (.js), TypeScript React (.tsx), and JavaScript React (.jsx) files.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes and reports findings. It does not modify, refactor, or write any code changes.

faq.question: Can I use this for backend code reviews?
faq.answer: No. This skill is specifically designed for frontend code. Use language-specific review tools for Python, Go, or other backend languages.

faq.question: How does this compare to ESLint?
faq.answer: This skill complements ESLint by providing contextual feedback on business logic, architecture decisions, and code patterns that automated tools cannot evaluate.

faq.question: Can I customize the review checklist?
faq.answer: The skill uses built-in reference documents (business-logic.md, code-quality.md, performance.md). Review focus can be adjusted through prompt templates.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Next.js and React?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes React-specific checks for hooks usage, component patterns, and framework-specific best practices.
